ticketTitle: Create a language-tier registry mapping every known language ID to an analysis capability tier (None/Generic/Lint/Parse/Full), with a test that fails CI when a new language extension is added to discovery without a tier entry.

deliverables.planText: Add an exported function KnownLanguages() []string to internal/discovery/generic.go. It must iterate over certifiableExts, collect all unique language-ID values into a set, sort them, and return the slice. The existing certifiableExts map is the single source of truth — do not duplicate or hardcode the list. Keep the implementation minimal: build a map[string]bool for dedup, extract keys, sort.Strings, return.
deliverables.objective: Add exported KnownLanguages() to internal/discovery/generic.go, deduplicating and sorting language IDs from certifiableExts
nonGoals: Do not change certifiableExts or any discovery logic
Do not add new language IDs
Do not touch any other file

deliverables.planText: Create internal/engine/language_tiers.go. Define a Tier int iota enum: TierNone (0), TierGeneric, TierLint, TierParse, TierFull. Add a String() method following the EvidenceKind pattern (map[Tier]string + fmt.Sprintf fallback). Define a package-level var languageTiers = map[string]Tier{} with all 22 language IDs: go/ts/js/py/rs → TierFull; rb/java/kt/sh/c/cpp/cs/swift/php/elixir/erlang/scala/lua/r/zig/sql/proto → TierGeneric. Implement func TierForLanguage(lang string) Tier that returns the map entry or TierGeneric for unknown languages. Bundle these together because the enum, registry, and helper form one cohesive abstraction.
deliverables.objective: Create internal/engine/language_tiers.go with Tier enum, static registry of 22 language→tier mappings, and TierForLanguage helper
nonGoals: Do not change scorer.go or any scoring logic
Do not modify init_cmd.go or report generation
Do not import discovery package — the registry is self-contained

deliverables.planText: Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go using package engine_test (external test package, matching existing convention). Import discovery and engine packages. Write three tests: (a) TestTierRegistry_AllKnownLanguagesHaveTiers — calls discovery.KnownLanguages(), asserts every returned ID has a non-zero entry when passed to engine.TierForLanguage; (b) TestTierRegistry_UnknownLanguageReturnsGeneric — asserts engine.TierForLanguage("nonsense-language") == engine.TierGeneric; (c) TestTierRegistry_SwiftIsGeneric — asserts engine.TierForLanguage("swift") == engine.TierGeneric as a regression pin. This test fails CI when a new extension is added to certifiableExts without a corresponding tier entry, meeting the issue's core requirement.
deliverables.objective: Create internal/engine/language_tiers_test.go with three tests: exhaustive registry coverage, unknown fallback, and Swift regression pin
nonGoals: Do not change any production code
